The organization maintains a 99.5% program expense ratio and a fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and domestic stability, balanced by a medium data confidence level and a focus on long-term capacity building rather than immediate crisis relief.
areas_for_improvement: Limited external data regarding specific long-term impact metrics beyond graduation rates
Moderate alignment for donors seeking high-urgency international relief, as the focus is domestic capacity building

dimension_explanations.alignment: ISF offers clear zakat-eligible giving opportunities for Muslim donors focusing on domestic education and community growth [2][5]. However, the alignment score is moderate because the cause is categorized as domestic poverty/capacity building rather than high-urgency humanitarian aid, which may result in a lower fit for some donor profiles [6].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high financial health with a total revenue of $12,484,881 and a 99.5% program expense ratio [4][6]. While it effectively builds community capacity through its graduating classes and scholarship distributions, the cost per beneficiary is relatively high at $13,220.62, reflecting the intensive nature of private academic instruction [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Services Foundation has been a cornerstone of Muslim community development and education since 1989, operating premier academic institutions in North Texas.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 99.5%, indicating nearly all funds go directly to services
Strong liquidity with 14.5 months of working capital available
Highly efficient fundraising operations with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised
Established track record of over 30 years in community service and education
baseline_narrative.summary: The Islamic Services Foundation (ISF) is a non-profit organization dedicated to serving the Muslim community through educational programs including Brighter Horizons Academy and Little Horizons Academy [1][6]. Based in Garland, Texas, the foundation manages significant community assets and provides financial aid to ensure educational accessibility [1][4].

confidence.notes: Strong financial reporting but governance transparency is a limiting factor.

alignment.explanation: The charity is <cite id="15">zakat-eligible</cite> and serves the <cite id="15">fuqara and masakin</cite> categories, primarily through educational financial aid and local relief.
alignment.improvement: The foundation could <cite id="15">clarify its specific zakat allocation policy</cite> to better inform donors on how funds are distributed across asnaf categories.

credibility.explanation: Donors can find confidence in ISF's <cite id="1">37-year track record</cite> and its management of <cite id="14">$15.9 million in total assets</cite>. However, credibility is tempered by <cite id="12">low board independence</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the foundation should <cite id="12">increase the percentage of independent board members</cite> and publish more detailed governance policies.

impact.explanation: The foundation delivers high-quality education with a <cite id="5">100.0% program ratio</cite>, though the <cite id="13">cost per beneficiary is approximately $13,220</cite>, typical for private academic institutions.
impact.improvement: Impact reporting could be improved by <cite id="10">separating restricted endowment funds</cite> from operating reserves to clarify available liquidity.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize long-term Islamic institutional building in the United States and value high financial efficiency in educational settings.
donor_motivations: Want to support established Islamic K-12 education
Value organizations with zero fundraising costs
Seek to provide zakat-eligible financial aid for students
giving_considerations: Donations support a large-scale institution with significant assets
Focus is on domestic capacity rather than international emergency relief
Governance is centralized with limited independent board oversight
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-impact international medical relief or those who prioritize organizations with high board independence.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified graduation rates, scholarship data, and relief beneficiary counts, though long-term longitudinal impact data is not publicly detailed.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: ISF creates impact by providing high-quality Islamic and academic education to produce community leaders, supported by financial aid to ensure socioeconomic diversity.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the high cost of private education translates into tangible student success and community leadership.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While ISF has strong reserves, the <cite id="11">23.2% growth rate</cite> and expanding student body require continuous capital for facility maintenance and financial aid.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="5">100.0% of its expenses to programs</cite> and maintains a <cite id="9">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>.
ISF maintains <cite id="10">14.5 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term institutional stability.
The foundation's flagship academy reported a <cite id="8">graduating class of 72 students</cite> with <cite id="8">8 students receiving full-ride scholarships</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Services Foundation exhibits a rare level of financial leaness for an institution of its size, reporting a <cite id="5">99.5% program expense ratio</cite> in its most recent filings. This efficiency is bolstered by a <cite id="11">3-year revenue CAGR of 23.2%</cite>, showing rapid growth in community support and service fees. Furthermore, the organization’s <cite id="10">13.9 to 14.5 months of operating reserves</cite> far exceeds the industry standard, ensuring that its educational institutions can withstand economic fluctuations while continuing to provide <cite id="8">significant financial aid</cite> to the community.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Services Foundation (ISF) has served as a cornerstone of Muslim community development and education in North Texas since its <cite id="1">founding in 1989</cite>. Based in Garland, the organization operates premier academic institutions including <cite id="2">Brighter Horizons Academy and Little Horizons Academy</cite>, providing a comprehensive Islamic educational environment. In 2023, the foundation demonstrated significant financial scale, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $14,235,728</cite>, a substantial portion of which is derived from <cite id="4">$10,415,037 in program service revenue</cite>. The organization maintains an <cite id="5">exceptional program expense ratio of 99.5%</cite>, indicating that virtually all functional expenses are directed toward its core educational and community missions. Beyond its primary schools, ISF manages the <cite id="6">ISF Qur’an Institute and various Sunday school programs</cite>, while also facilitating humanitarian efforts such as <cite id="7">medical missions and urgent relief</cite> which reached over 30,000 beneficiaries in recent years. To ensure accessibility, the foundation distributed <cite id="8">$1,000,000 in financial aid</cite> to support students and families within its network.
